title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, 47, will run the Boston Marathon with friend Peggy for Mass General after surviving breast cancer.
Both women were diagnosed with breast cancer six months apart and are running to celebrate life and survivorship.
Gannon had to postpone her participation due to surgery setbacks but will run her eighth marathon on April 20, 2026.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted SNL's 16th episode of the 51st season on April 4, marking his fifth hosting.
Musical guest Jack White performed for the fifth time on SNL, accompanied by a team of dancers.
Black joined the five-timers club with a sketch featuring Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, Candice Bergen, and Melissa McCarthy.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown admits past criticisms of his left hand were partly true due to a left wrist injury.
Brown had major left wrist surgery in 2021 and a reinjury in 2023, affecting his dribbling ability.
Since 2023, Brown shows improvement, achieving career-high 28.7 points, 7 rebounds, and 5.3 assists per game.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo visited Boston for a Padres game, marking over a decade since leaving his role with NESN.
Orsillo shared his fondness for his former job with the Red Sox, calling it his 'dream job'.
Current NESN colleagues, like Jim Rice, showed support, and Orsillo received warm greetings from Boston fans.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora is using Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to defensive concerns early this season.
Anthony has had defensive struggles, but contributed offensively with a triple and a key hit against the Padres.
The Red Sox have a disappointing 2-6 record as they face the Padres for a rubber match at Fenway Park.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two runs on three hits and four walks over four innings against the Padres.
Despite his struggles, Early matched the Red Sox record for strikeouts in the first six career games with 39.
The Red Sox lost to the Padres 3-2 after closer Aroldis Chapman allowed the winning run in the ninth inning.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution won 3-0 against CF Montreal, with goals by Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card in the 34th minute for leaving his technical area.
Luca Langoni contributed a goal and an assist, showcasing improved form and leading MLS in assists with five.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas will resume hitting from a tee Monday after a rib injury; recovery not expected to be long-term.
Johan Oviedo to meet with Tommy John surgeon Tuesday due to a right elbow strain affecting his pitching.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval nearing minor league rehab assignments after missing recent seasons due to injuries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her doctors and MGH's AYA program.
Taylor was diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, treated by specialists at Mass General, impacting her college life.
She credits MGH's survivorship program for support and is running to raise awareness and gratitude for cancer patients.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Boston Medical Center's social workers.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 for Team BMC, highlighting social workers' crucial role in patient care and crisis management.
Training includes events like HandleBar classes and Stats Bar fundraisers to gather support for BMC's community healthcare mission.
